user_query:
I'm an EMT dealing with chronic stress, and I want to create a supportive social media carousel for my peers that is grounded in my own experience and points to credible help. Using the files under input/, please do the following and produce the deliverables under output/:

Inputs to use:
- input/journal_entries.jsonl (my anonymized stress journal)
- input/community_guidelines.md (tone and crisis disclaimer)

Required deliverables:
1) output/summary.json
   - Build this by reading and analyzing input/journal_entries.jsonl.
   - Include fields:
     {
       "total_entries": <int>,
       "top_stressors": [
         {"name": <string>, "count": <int>, "sample_quote": <string max 20 words>},
         {"name": <string>, "count": <int>, "sample_quote": <string max 20 words>},
         {"name": <string>, "count": <int>, "sample_quote": <string max 20 words>}
       ],
       "coping_activities": [
         {"name": <string>, "count": <int>},
         {"name": <string>, "count": <int>}
       ]
     }
   - Rules:
     • Identify the top 3 stressors by frequency across entries. For each, include one representative quote from notes (<=20 words).
     • Identify the 2 most frequent coping activities.
     • Use the exact lowercase strings found in the JSONL for stressor and coping activity names.

2) output/resources.json
   - Perform search engine queries on the Internet to find exactly 3 official, publicly available mental health support resources that specifically serve first responders/EMS. Pick only from government agencies or well-known national nonprofits. Do not rely on local files for this.
   - For each resource, extract and save the following fields:
     {"id": "R1|R2|R3", "organization": <string>, "resource_name": <string>, "audience": "first responders/EMS", "support_type": "hotline"|"peer support"|"program"|"therapy locator", "contact": <phone number or text code if available>, "description": <one-sentence plain-language summary>, "source_org_category": "government"|"nonprofit", "last_checked": <ISO date YYYY-MM-DD>}
   - The array must contain exactly three objects with ids R1, R2, R3.
   - Also include a top-level array field "search_queries" listing the queries you used.

3) output/carousel.md
   - Draft a 5-slide carousel titled: EMT Stress Support: You're Not Alone
   - Structure:
     Slide 1: A brief hook that acknowledges EMT stress (<=60 words).
     Slide 2: Summarize the 3 top stressors from output/summary.json by name (use the exact lowercase names) and 1–2 sentences validating the experience (<=60 words).
     Slide 3: Mention the 2 coping activities from output/summary.json by their exact lowercase names and give encouraging, non-clinical guidance (<=60 words).
     Slide 4: List the three resources by resource_name and contact, each with an inline citation tag [R1], [R2], [R3] that matches ids in output/resources.json (<=60 words total or split by lines).
     Slide 5: A closing call-to-action for peer support and include the exact Crisis Disclaimer line from input/community_guidelines.md (<=60 words before the disclaimer). Include the disclaimer verbatim.
   - Tone must be peer-supportive and non-clinical per input/community_guidelines.md. Avoid diagnostic/prescriptive language.

Cross-checks to satisfy:
- The stressor and coping names used in carousel.md must exactly match the strings in output/summary.json.
- The [R1]/[R2]/[R3] references in carousel.md must correspond to ids in output/resources.json, and each referenced resource must appear there.
- The Crisis Disclaimer line in carousel.md must exactly match the line in input/community_guidelines.md.

Notes:
- Keep each slide under the stated word limits.
- Do not include identifiable details from the journal; sample quotes in summary.json should be brief and generalized.
- You may include phone/text contacts for resources if available, but do not include direct URLs in the carousel.

Please save only the three deliverables to the specified output paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
